rationale: The page provides detailed information on ISRO's analog research station in Tso Kar Valley, the upcoming Lunar Module Launch Vehicle (LMLV), and a comparative overview of ISRO's main launch vehicles (PSLV, GSLV, LVM 3, SSLV). It also includes a news update on the PSLV-C61 mission failure.
cleanedText:
• Led by: ISRO’s Human Space Flight Centre (HSFC), with industry partners and top Indian institutions.
• Objectives
o Simulates extraterrestrial environments like the Moon and Mars.
o Enables testing of human survival, health protocols, and equipment in space-like environments.
• Studies to be conducted: Epigenetics, genomics, physiology, psychology, Sample collection and microbial analysis techniques, etc.
• Aimed at preparing for long-term extra-terrestrial human missions.
• Other similar research stations across the World: Mars Desert Research Station of the US, Flashline Mars Arctic Station of Canada, and BIOS-3 of Russia.
Why Tso Kar Valley was chosen?
• Mars-like conditions: High UV radiation, Low atmospheric pressure, Extreme cold and Saline permafrost
• Supports both technological tests and astrobiology research.
4.13. LUNAR MODULE LAUNCH VEHICLE (LMLV)
Why in the News?
ISRO’s heaviest rocket Lunar Module Launch Vehicle (LMLV) will be ready by 2035.
Key Features of LMLV
• Design: Improved version of the NGLV (Next Generation Launch Vehicle).
• Purpose: Lunar missions, including India's first human mission to the Moon, planned by 2040.
• Three Stages Involved: 3 Stages: Liquid propellant for its first two stages and a cryogenic propellant for its third stage.
ISRO's Key Launch Vehicles and Capabilities
• PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle): ISRO's workhorse (due to its consistent performance), a third-generation vehicle, capable of launching satellites into Sun-synchronous Polar Orbit, LEO, GTO (e.g., Chandrayaan-1, Mars Orbiter Mission).
o It is a four-stage vehicle and first Indian launch vehicle to be equipped with liquid stages.
• GSLV (Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle): A fourth-generation, three-stage vehicle designed to launch 2.0-ton class satellites into GTO for communication satellites.
• LVM 3: A heavy-lift, three-stage vehicle capable of carrying 4-ton class satellites to GTO or about 10 tons to LEO.
o It is proposed to be used for Bharatiya Antariksh Station.
o Recently, Godrej Enterprises delivered the first human-rated L110 stage Vikas engine for Gaganyaan project to ISRO.
• SSLV (Small Satellite Launch Vehicle): Three-stage, all-solid propulsion vehicle designed for launching Mini, Micro, or Nano satellites.
Related News
PSLV-C61/EOS-09 Mission
• ISRO’s 63rd PSLV mission (PSLV-C61/EOS-09) failed due to a technical malfunction during the third stage of the PSLV rocket.
o Since its first flight, PSLV has failed only twice.
• About PSLV-C61/EOS-09 Mission
o EOS-09 is an advanced Earth observation satellite, equipped with C-band SAR technology.
o Objective: To provide continuous and reliable remote sensing data for operational applications across various sectors.
